programmes.show_description: The Bates. Paul Mackie and his expert team must haul a crew of first-time gold miners out of an epic three-year gold drought before their operation and family legacy ends in ruin. S1, Ep1
Secrets Of Skull Grave. Archaeologists use state-of-the-art digital technology to uncover the mystery behind human skulls from Chile. What happened to these discarded skulls? S1, Ep1
War Of The Ancients. Archaeologists use new digital technology to try and solve the mystery behind an ancient burial ground filled with bones, swords, an axe and more in Germany. S1, Ep2
Everyday items go under the spotlight. How pharmaceutical blister packs, deli slicers and weather vanes are made. S18, Ep11
Top and bowler hats, solar water heaters, sticky buns and electrostatic speakers go under the microscope. S18, Ep12
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as electric boats and fencing foils made? S26, Ep26
Follow the production process of aluminium foil, snowboards, soft polymer contact lenses and bread and find out how each of these items is made. S1, Ep1

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Max McMurdo and Simon O'Brien travel across the UK to find unique items lost in people's homes and fix them up for a profit. They turn trash into treasure! S3, Ep23

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Max McMurdo and Simon O'Brien travel across the UK to find unique items lost in people's homes and fix them up for a profit. They turn trash into treasure! S3, Ep25
Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer begin their hunt for antiques in Stow-on-the-Wold. Izzie buys a toast rack, while Charlie eyes a military first aid kit. S20, Ep6
Izzie Balmer and Charlie Ross hunt for hidden gems in Gloucester. Plus, Izzie learns about the incredible story of Britain's first female astronomer. S20, Ep7
Izzie Balmer and Charlie Ross continue their road trip in Hartley Wintney. Charlie buys a cut glass sugar shaker, while Izzie eyes three bronze pigs. S20, Ep8
Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer search for interesting items in Salisbury. Plus, Izzie travels to Selborne to learn about one of the world's first ecologists. S20, Ep9
Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer scour the Isle of Wight for profitable antiques. Plus, Charlie heads to Blackgang to explore Britain's oldest amusement park. S20, Ep10
First up, the team bring a well-loved Indian guitar back to life. Plus, leather expert Suzie is challenged with restoring a vintage RAF-issued flying jacket. S6, Ep17
Silversmith Brenton is challenged with restoring a battered, old trophy. Plus, wood expert Will revives an impressive birdcage with a remarkable backstory. S6, Ep18
Metal expert Dominic restores a 1940's snail riding toy. Plus, silversmith Brenton works on a treasured trophy won in a 1949 ballroom dancing competition. S6, Ep19
Angus is in Newcastle to clear out a home that's full of books. He takes an interest in a Bechstein piano, a scary vase and a silver loving cup. S1, Ep4
Sacred Secret & Saucy Surprise. Angus sells the treasures of an Abbey to fund the monks' retirement, and a motoring fanatic's cheeky sculpture sets pulses racing on sale day. S6, Ep20
Susannah Constantine & Leslie Ash. Angus raids TV presenter Susannah's couture stash, then helps actress Leslie uncover treasures, memories, and a few famous surprises! S5, Ep4
Metal expert Dominic gives a rare, old pedal aeroplane a new lease of life. Plus, luthier Julian works on a ukulele made from a box of cigars in 1926. S6, Ep20
An ancestor of famous philosopher Simone de Beauvoir brings in a painting for a new lick of paint, and a beloved silver bracelet gets its charm back. S8, Ep57
First up, the team bring a well-loved Indian guitar back to life. Plus, leather expert Suzie is challenged with restoring a vintage RAF-issued flying jacket. S6, Ep17
Silversmith Brenton is challenged with restoring a battered, old trophy. Plus, wood expert Will revives an impressive birdcage with a remarkable backstory. S6, Ep18
Metal expert Dominic restores a 1940's snail riding toy. Plus, silversmith Brenton works on a treasured trophy won in a 1949 ballroom dancing competition. S6, Ep19
Around the world, there are extraordinary building mistakes. Design teams and experts analyse what went wrong and how engineers are fixing them. S5, Ep10
A series of explosions threatens downtown Dallas; a pilot is trapped after a freak helicopter accident; a biker survives a crash at 200mph; and a typhoon strikes Japan. S1, Ep5
A pilot fights to escape a helicopter accident, a powerboat spins out of control, a tanker fire erupts into an 1,800 degree fireball and a plane crashes from the sky. S1, Ep6
Terrorists bomb artillery stockpiles in Addis Ababa; jets crash at an air show; a bulldozer wreaks havoc on a small town; and a mansion is swallowed by a sinkhole. S1, Ep8
A helicopter pilot misjudges his moves at an air show; a fighter jet test flight ends in disaster; skydivers escape a burning plane; and a racer loses control at 278mph. S1, Ep9
Follow the production process of compact discs, mozzarella cheese and tights and find out how each of these items is made. S1, Ep2
Follow the production process of apple juice, cosmetics and steel, and find out how each of these items is made. S1, Ep3

An ancestor of famous philosopher Simone de Beauvoir brings in a painting for a new lick of paint, and a beloved silver bracelet gets its charm back. S8, Ep57
Different Visions. Drew meets dealers who see antiques in different ways. He finds a carved lion with a mysterious back-story, and four chairs by his design hero, Pugin. S18, Ep3
Shop Local. Drew seeks out antiques that are rich with local provenance. On the Scottish border, he finds a Georgian Cockpen chair, then snaps up a chilling art piece in North Wales. S18, Ep4
As the season ends, the Dirt Dogs must deliver $233,000 of gold to hit the target. Alex faces a shock resignation, and the Gold Gypsies struggle with more gold thieves. S3, Ep13
The Dirt Dogs invest in a hard rock mining plant. And, the Gold Gypsies are eager to be back in the field, but a $20,000 excavator repair is a tough start to the season. S4, Ep1
Irita Marriott brings Latvian flair to the game as she takes on Tim Medhurst in a sporting tour of Lincolnshire's antiques emporia. One expert has a brush with the law. S22, Ep6
In the seaside town of Filey, Angus and the team help a couple clear out their home. Angus sees potential in some vintage toys and boxes of old soap. S1, Ep6
An ancestor of famous philosopher Simone de Beauvoir brings in a painting for a new lick of paint, and a beloved silver bracelet gets its charm back. S8, Ep57
Stumpie's American Take Away. In the New Forest, Hampshire, Henry and Sam visit a collector of Americana. What rare bicycles and classic sportsters will they find in his sheds? S2, Ep7
Chiller Thriller. In the Midlands, Henry and Sam visit the sheds of a bike builder. He has a collection of rare bikes, but it's the surprise in his freezer that attracts the duo. S2, Ep9
Neil. In Hertfordshire, Henry and Fuzz meet Neil, who boasts a shed full of Japanese bikes, cars, and campervans. What will they come away with? S6, Ep11
Alan. Henry and Fuzz meet collector extraordinaire, Alan in Norfolk. They search through rare Harleys, British bikes, hot rods and piles of automotive junk. S6, Ep12
The Vast Auction Hero. Angus helps a woman turn her collection into a mind-boggling sum at auction, and he has an opportunity to score with some rare football memorabilia. S6, Ep8
Whisky And Watercolours. Angus empties two homes packed to the rafters with exquisite antiques, intriguing curios and long-forgotten bottles of whisky. S6, Ep9
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit. S25, Ep6

programmes.show_name: New: Shed And Buried: Classic Helicopter
programmes.show_description: Henry tackles his biggest project, restoring a Bell 47 helicopter for the 80th Anniversary Flight. It's a race against time to get it off the ground.
Dara Ó Briain is joined by guests Rhys James, Alasdair Beckett-King, Ed Byrne, Hugh Dennis and Ellie Taylor to comedically examine the week's events. S1, Ep7
How do they turn trees into toilet paper? How do they make absinthe and how do they make cricket balls tough enough to face batsmen? S10, Ep3
How do they cultivate green salad on scorching desert plains, how do they make rigging for ships and how do they produce lifesaving insulin from bread? S10, Ep4
